rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Source URL + type label — mandatory; labels live in maintainer block only.
Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
Curran org-size claim: '500 people' vs. article's ~473 R&D / ~1,305 total.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:24 — 'The org is 500 people.' WebFetch of ideas.fin.ai/p/2x-nine-months-later returns R&D org ~473; total Intercom ~1,305. Maintainer-block source-verification section already flags this number for pre-cohort verification and correction. Non-blocking: the rounding (473 → 500) is within the maintainer's stated verification scope; no unverified-stat flag needed because the URL is open and the number is being checked.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Pre-cohort: open the Curran URL, confirm the R&D figure, and update '500 people' to the verified number (likely ~473 or the article's stated framing).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Audit scope derives from TRAININGS registry + link-walk, not disk dir.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude Code ships three scheduling primitives. Desktop local tasks (invoked from the Schedule sidebar) for standing work on your laptop. `/loop` for in-session repetition. `/schedule` for cloud-backed Routines.

The [Long-running shapes section in Claude Code for engineers](../trainings/agentic-engineering-101/reference/claude-code-for-engineers.md) walks through the four primitives, when each fits, and how a skill plugs into each.
Opus 4.7 will be Opus 4.8, then something with a different name.

citations_checked.claim: Darragh Curran, Intercom — 19.2% auto-approved PRs, 14.6 min vs 75.8 min, 86% ≤20 lines, org is 500 people
Ronacher's three pieces: reference, plan, verifier
